name: PATHWAY_COMBINATIONS_FOR_DOUBLE_HIGH_EXPRESSION_TARGETPAIRS
description: Pathway combinations for double high expression target pairs. Auto-generated from the core-four benchmark and aligned to the validated query-frame Cypher semantics.
cypher:
MATCH (tp:TargetPair)-[:HAS_TARGET]->(t1:Target)-[:DIFFERENTIAL_AND_HIGHLY_EXPRESSED_IN]->(c:Cancer {name: $cancer})
MATCH (tp)-[:HAS_TARGET]->(t2:Target)-[:DIFFERENTIAL_AND_HIGHLY_EXPRESSED_IN]->(c)
WHERE t1 <> t2
OPTIONAL MATCH (t1)-[:IN_PATHWAY]->(pw1:Pathway)
OPTIONAL MATCH (t2)-[:IN_PATHWAY]->(pw2:Pathway)
RETURN DISTINCT tp.name AS target_pair, collect(DISTINCT pw1.name) AS target1_pathways, collect(DISTINCT pw2.name) AS target2_pathways
ORDER BY target_pair
LIMIT 10

examples: For target pairs whose two targets are both highly expressed in DLBC, what pathway combinations do they represent?
For target pairs whose two targets are both highly expressed in THCA, what pathway combinations do they represent?
For target pairs whose two targets are both highly expressed in KICH, what pathway combinations do they represent?
